meta.description: Curated commuter-ready frontier media — YouTube videos, blog posts, and audio-compatible content aligned to current learning phase. All items are passively digestible during transit, walking, gym, or meals.
meta.safetyNote: All URLs are public. No login-required content. No scraping. YouTube links are direct public video URLs only.

media.title: 3Blue1Brown — Essence of Linear Algebra (Full Playlist)
media.activeRecallPrompt: After each video: close your eyes and try to describe the geometric intuition in one sentence. What *is* a matrix transformation?
media.why_now: This is the canonical visual companion to Week 4 linear algebra. Every concept in the playlist maps 1:1 to your current study material.
media.implementation_note: Watch one chapter per commute day. Takes 16 commutes to finish. Do NOT binge — space it out to reinforce weekly topics.

media.title: Andrej Karpathy — The spelled-out intro to neural networks and backpropagation: building micrograd
media.activeRecallPrompt: What is a computational graph? How does a single scalar backward pass know which operations led to the final loss?
media.why_now: Watching now (passive audio) builds conceptual scaffolding for Month 3. You will understand gradient descent 2× faster when you get there because you heard the story first.
media.implementation_note: Break into 3 sessions of ~45 min each. First pass: audio only on commute. Second pass: screen + code when you reach Month 3.

media.title: Simon Willison — Everything I know about LLM prompting
media.activeRecallPrompt: Name three prompting patterns Simon uses. How does system prompt vs. user prompt affect model behavior?
media.why_now: API patterns (Week 3) are the foundation for prompt engineering. This post is the practical playbook. Read now as a preview of where Week 3 leads.
media.implementation_note: Read once during a lunch break. Bookmark the prompt patterns for implementation in Month 2.

media.title: Sebastian Raschka — Understanding and Coding the Self-Attention Mechanism
media.activeRecallPrompt: What are Q, K, and V in attention? Why do you divide by sqrt(d_k)?
media.why_now: Parked for Month 4+ implementation. Commuter passive read now is fine to build schema — you will understand why after Month 2 embeddings and Month 3 backprop.
media.implementation_note: Audio-style read only for now. Do NOT attempt to code along until Month 4. Return with full context then.

media.title: fast.ai Lesson 1 — Practical Deep Learning for Coders (2024)
media.activeRecallPrompt: What is the 'top-down' approach to learning ML? Why does Jeremy Howard argue you should train a model before understanding the math?
media.why_now: Month 3 preparation. Jeremy's philosophy complements the bottom-up math approach you're doing in Month 1. Watching now balances the perspective — you're doing both math-first AND seeing what the destination looks like.
media.implementation_note: One gym session watch. Note your questions — bring them into Month 3 study.

media.title: DeepLearning.AI — The Batch (May 2026 issue)
media.activeRecallPrompt: What is the one most relevant engineering story from this week's Batch? How does it connect to something you're currently studying?
media.why_now: Weekly 10-minute frontier scan budget. Andrew Ng's editorial lens is educationally calibrated — high signal, low hype, practical framing.
media.implementation_note: Read every Thursday. Budget exactly 10 minutes. If an article needs more time, add it to LATER.md and move on.
